headline: IREN’s 5.8 GW Buildout And Nvidia Deal Reframe AI Growth Story
summary: IREN (NasdaqGS:IREN) has expanded its global data center development pipeline to 5.8 GW, signaling a much larger buildout of high-performance computing sites. The company has entered a multi year, $3.4b partnership with Nvidia to supply AI infrastructure, adding a major new customer relationship alongside its existing work with Microsoft. These moves indicate a deeper push into AI cloud infrastructure and a broader geographic footprint for IREN’s computing assets. IREN’s share price sits at...

headline: Microsoft Azure Cuts In China Highlight Regulatory And AI Rebalancing
summary: Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT) is cutting several hundred roles in its Azure cloud unit in China. The move is described as a response to rising regulatory pressure on data and cloud services in the country. The changes affect part of a core business area for Microsoft, as Azure underpins its cloud and AI offerings. For investors following NasdaqGS:MSFT, this development sits at the intersection of cloud computing, AI infrastructure and geopolitics. Azure is central to the company’s broader cloud...

headline: Oracle Stock Slides on Mixed Earnings. Software Is an Issue.
summary: Oracle  shares fell in the Thursday premarket after the company reported mixed earnings results for its fiscal fourth quarter after the Wednesday bell.  Oracle also said that its capital expenditures for the current fiscal year would be around $70 billion, plus another $20 billion to $25 billion paid directly by customers.  In its just ended fiscal 2026 year, Oracle’s capex was $56 billion, up from $21 billion in fiscal 2025.

content.title: Oracle Stock Slides on Mixed Earnings. Software Is an Issue.
content.summary: Oracle  shares fell in the Thursday premarket after the company reported mixed earnings results for its fiscal fourth quarter after the Wednesday bell.  Oracle also said that its capital expenditures for the current fiscal year would be around $70 billion, plus another $20 billion to $25 billion paid directly by customers.  In its just ended fiscal 2026 year, Oracle’s capex was $56 billion, up from $21 billion in fiscal 2025.

headline: Oracle stock sinking as its AI spending climbs, debt grows
summary: Oracle (ORCL) stock has dropped by as much as 10% in Thursday's pre-market trading despite topping fiscal fourth quarter earnings and revenue estimates. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Monetary Matters Network co-founder Jack Farley for a conversation about whether the AI infrastructure company is showing signs of weakness or oversaturation in the buildout landscape.

content.title: Oracle stock sinking as its AI spending climbs, debt grows
content.description:
Oracle (ORCL) stock has dropped by as much as 10% in Thursday's pre-market trading despite topping fiscal fourth quarter earnings and revenue estimates.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Monetary Matters Network co-founder Jack Farley for a conversation about whether the AI infrastructure company is showing signs of weakness or oversaturation in the buildout landscape.
content.summary: Oracle (ORCL) stock has dropped by as much as 10% in Thursday's pre-market trading despite topping fiscal fourth quarter earnings and revenue estimates. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman is joined by Yahoo Finance Tech Editor Dan Howley and Monetary Matters Network co-founder Jack Farley for a conversation about whether the AI infrastructure company is showing signs of weakness or oversaturation in the buildout landscape.

headline: Google spurned U.S. quantum fund over conditions that would slow R&D
summary: Google Quantum AI COO Charina Chou confirmed the decision at the Semafor Tech Summit in San Francisco on June 10, saying the company walked away because of "various conditions that came with the funding" that would have prevented it from moving at its desired pace toward building a functional quantum computer. The disclosure sheds new light on why Google — alongside Microsoft and IonQ — was conspicuously absent from the Trump administration's list of nine quantum recipients announced last month.

content.title: Google spurned U.S. quantum fund over conditions that would slow R&D
content.summary: Google Quantum AI COO Charina Chou confirmed the decision at the Semafor Tech Summit in San Francisco on June 10, saying the company walked away because of "various conditions that came with the funding" that would have prevented it from moving at its desired pace toward building a functional quantum computer. The disclosure sheds new light on why Google — alongside Microsoft and IonQ — was conspicuously absent from the Trump administration's list of nine quantum recipients announced last month.

headline: Can CRWD Stock Deliver If The AI Boom Stalls?
summary: After a 40% run over the last 12 months, the conversation around CrowdStrike (CRWD) stock is almost entirely about artificial intelligence. You hear about the Mythos inflection point and a new generation of products designed to secure AI deployments. But with that excitement comes a fair question from skeptics: Is this a durable trend, or just a surge of interest and intent from companies still in the early innings of actual AI deployment.

content.title: Can CRWD Stock Deliver If The AI Boom Stalls?
content.summary: After a 40% run over the last 12 months, the conversation around CrowdStrike (CRWD) stock is almost entirely about artificial intelligence. You hear about the Mythos inflection point and a new generation of products designed to secure AI deployments. But with that excitement comes a fair question from skeptics: Is this a durable trend, or just a surge of interest and intent from companies still in the early innings of actual AI deployment.
